    Enum CadEntityAttribute

    Entities enum

    Namespace: Aspose.CAD.FileFormats.Cad
    Assembly: Aspose.CAD.dll
    Syntax
    [ComVisible(false)]
public enum CadEntityAttribute

    Entity type

    Cad001

    Entity attribute value

    The Cad 003.

    Cad006

    Linetype name (present if not BYLAYER). The special name BYBLOCK indicates a floating linetype (optional)

    The Cad 038.

    Cad039

    Thickness (optional; default = 0)

    Cad040

    Radius or width

    Cad041

    Default end width (optional; default = 0)

    Cad042

    Bulge (optional; default is 0). The bulge is the tangent of one fourth the included angle for an arc segment, made negative if the arc goes clockwise from the start point to the endpoint. A bulge of 0 indicates a straight segment, and a bulge of 1 is a semicircle

    Cad043

    Control Point Tolerance

    Cad044

    Block Attribute Width

    Cad045

    Line Spacing Factor

    Cad046

    Block Content Rotation

    Cad047

    Block Transformation Matrix

    Cad048

    Linetype scale (optional)

    Cad049

    The Cad049

    Cad050

    First angle or Rotation angle or...

    Cad051

    First angle or Rotation angle or...

    Cad052

    The Cad 052.

    Cad053

    The Cad fifty three

    Cad060

    Object visibility (optional): 0 = Visible; 1 = Invisible

    Cad061

    The Cad 061.

    Cad062

    Color number (present if not BYLAYER);

    Cad063

    Indicator color

    Cad064

    The Cad 064.

    Cad065

    The Cad 065.

    Cad066

    Obsolete; formerly an “entities follow flag” (optional; ignore if present)

    Cad067

    Absent or zero indicates entity is in model space. 1 indicates entity is in paper space (optional)

    Cad068

    The Cad 068.

    Cad069

    The Cad 069.

    Cad070

    Vertex flags

    Cad071

    Polygon mesh M vertex count (optional; default = 0)

    Cad072

    Polygon mesh N vertex count (optional; default = 0)

    Cad073

    Smooth surface M density (optional; default = 0)

    Cad074

    Smooth surface N density (optional; default = 0)

    Cad075

    Curves and smooth surface type (optional; default = 0); integer codes, not bit-coded:

    Cad092

    Cad411

    The Cad 411.

    Cad420

    A 24-bit color value that should be dealt with in terms of bytes with values of 0 to 255. The lowest byte is the blue value, the middle byte is the green value, and the third byte is the red value. The top byte is always 0
